meaning: n.火花,闪烁,细微的颗粒或痕迹
examples: There is not a scintilla of truth in what she says.
He says there is "not a scintilla of evidence" to link him to any controversy.
There is not a scintilla of evidence in this case.
No-one with a scintilla of intelligence just presumes an idea like that will succeed.
There is not one scintilla of hope they will provide you with the appreciation you feel you deserve.
